result:
## Method note

`os_malloc()` is defined in `src/utils/os_unix.c:677`, but that definition is only active when `WPA_TRACE` is set. In the active `compile_commands.json` config, `WPA_TRACE` is **not** defined, so os.h instead makes `os_malloc(s)` a macro (`#define os_malloc(s) malloc((s))`, `os_free(p)` → `free((p))`) — confirmed via `LSP hover` on a call site. This also means none of the 8 caller files (`eap_fast_common.c`, `eap_eke_common.c`, `eap_gpsk_common.c`, `eap_ikev2_common.c`, `eap_sake_common.c`, `eap_pwd_common.c`, `eap_sim_common.c`, `ikev2_common.c`) are in `compile_commands.json` at all — only `eap_common.c`, `eap_peap_common.c`, `chap.c` are, and none of those call `os_malloc`. So this is an **LSP blind spot** (out-of-compile-DB files): I grepped for every `os_malloc(` call in `src/eap_common/` (26 sites) and manually traced each enclosing function's full control flow via `Read`.

## Findings — all 26 call sites, all correctly matched

**eap_fast_common.c**
- L101 `eap_fast_derive_key`: freed on error (L106); on success `out` is **returned** (ownership transfer to caller) — correct.

**eap_eke_common.c**
- L350 `eap_eke_derive_key`: freed on error (L359) and success (L362). OK.
- L484 `eap_eke_derive_ke_ki`(ish): freed on error (L492) and success (L501). OK.
- L525 `eap_eke_derive_ka`: freed on error (L540) and success (L543). OK.
- L570 `eap_eke_derive_msk`: freed on error (L586) and success (L589). OK.

**eap_gpsk_common.c**
- L140 `eap_gpsk_derive_keys_helper`: freed on error (L157) and success (L160), before later unrelated returns. OK.
- L298 `eap_gpsk_derive_keys`: single unconditional `os_free(seed)` at L336 after the switch, before the sole `return ret`. Covers all cipher branches. OK.
- L371 `eap_gpsk_derive_mid_helper`: freed on error (L389) and success (L392). OK.
- L442 `eap_gpsk_derive_session_id`: single unconditional `os_free(seed)` at L469 before the sole return. OK.

**eap_ikev2_common.c**
- L31 `eap_ikev2_derive_keymat`: freed on error (L39) and success (L42). OK.

**eap_sake_common.c**
- L328 `eap_sake_compute_mic`: single alloc/single free (L372) before the only return. OK.

**eap_pwd_common.c**
- L159 `prfbuf` (in the PWE-finding function): freed unconditionally at L281, reached both via the `goto fail:` label and the normal fall-through — single free point covers every exit. OK.
- L297 `cruft` in `compute_keys`: freed on both early-return error paths (L307, L324) and on the success path (L331), before later unrelated returns. OK.

**eap_sim_common.c**
- L178 `eap_sim_verify_mac`: single alloc/free (L199) before sole return. OK.
- L373 `eap_sim_verify_mac_sha256`: same pattern (free L394). OK.
- L946 `eap_sim_parse_encr`: freed on both error paths (L952, L962); on success `decrypted` is **returned** (ownership transfer — callers are in `eap_peer/`, `eap_server/`, outside the requested scope). Correct as designed.

**ikev2_common.c**
- L404 `sign_data` in `ikev2_derive_auth_data`: freed on error (L420) and success (L423). OK.
- L496 `decrypted` in `ikev2_decrypt_payload`: freed on both error paths (L502, L510); returned (ownership transfer) on success (L517). OK.
- L651 `keybuf` in `ikev2_derive_sk_keys`: freed on error (L657) and unconditionally at L718 on the success path before later returns. OK.
- L663/671/679/687/695/703/711 (`keys->SK_d/SK_ai/SK_ar/SK_ei/SK_er/SK_pi/SK_pr`): each stored into the long-lived `keys` struct rather than freed locally. If any of the 7 allocations fails, `ikev2_keys_set()` (L720) detects it and `ikev2_free_keys()` (L616-627, uses `os_free` on all 7, NULL-safe) is called on the failure path (L721-722) — cleans up whichever succeeded. On overall success, cleanup is the caller's responsibility via the same `ikev2_free_keys()`. No leak on any path.

## Conclusion
All 26 `os_malloc()` calls reachable from `src/eap_common/` have a correctly matched `os_free()` (or macro-equivalent `free()`) on every exit path, including error paths. The only calls without a free *inside* the same function are the four where the buffer is deliberately returned to the caller (`eap_fast_derive_key`, `eap_sim_parse_encr`, `ikev2_decrypt_payload`) or stored into a caller-owned struct freed via `ikev2_free_keys()` — all intentional ownership-transfer patterns, not leaks.
